title: Ratel Configuration Schema
description: Schema for ratel.json — the project-level configuration file for the Ratel AI Software Factory. Documents model fields, fallback chains, and provider prefix conventions.

orchestrator.description: Configuration for the Orchestrator agent level (also used by research, smart-friend, and contract subagents).

model.description: Model slug in 'provider/model-id' format. null means 'use SDK default'. Must be a valid model from the Pi/OpenCode model registry. Provider aliases (e.g. 'openai') are normalized to canonical provider namespaces (e.g. 'openai-codex') on write.

fallbackModels.description: Ordered fallback model chain. Each entry must be a valid 'provider/model-id' slug. Duplicates (after alias normalization) are removed. Unknown slugs are warned and filtered at config-read time.

model.description: Model slug in 'provider/model-id' format. null means 'use SDK default'.

validators.description: Configuration for Validator agent spawns (scrutiny, code-review, user-testing).

model.description: Model slug in 'provider/model-id' format. null means 'use SDK default'.

providerPrefixes.description: Known provider prefixes for model slugs. These are the canonical provider namespaces used by Pi/OpenCode. Aliases (e.g. 'openai' for 'openai-codex') are normalized on write but discouraged in ratel.json.

examples.description: OpenAI models via OpenCode's codex provider. The alias 'openai' is normalized to 'openai-codex' when the target model exists.
OpenRouter — model IDs may contain slashes (e.g. 'openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.1')

providerPrefixes.note: Use canonical provider namespaces from the model registry (run list_models to see available providers). Avoid aliases in ratel.json — they are normalized on write but may cause confusion.
